notes: Both versions pass all assertions at 1.0 — the upstream-symbol violation was already partially caught by 'Bad comments: caller-specific knowledge' in the old skill.

Key qualitative difference: new skill explicitly names this as a dependency-direction architectural violation with a concrete test ('would this break if renamed?'), old skill treats it as comment quality decay. Framing matters for severity calibration.
No false positives on the clean-comment case in either version — the new rule is precise enough not to over-fire.

These evals don't differentiate versions quantitatively at 1.0 pass rate. Stronger discriminating evals would test edge cases where the old framing leads to wrong severity (e.g., Minor instead of Important).
